grounding_source_rows: human_disagreements row 28; paper failure mode injury_location
grounding_note: Disagreement row 28 (freezer slip-and-fall on workers' comp asking about 'personal injury options') split the human raters between Workers' Comp > State alone and adding Third Party Litigation. Whether an outside party caused the on-the-job injury is a fact users hold but rarely volunteer, and it changes the exact route from a comp-only claim to a comp-plus-third-party case.

scenarios.opening_query: I got hurt at work three months ago and I'm on workers' comp, but the checks don't cover my bills.
scenarios.hidden_fact: A delivery driver from another company backed his truck into me on our loading dock.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It was a delivery driver from a different company who backed into me on our dock.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether someone other than the employer or a coworker caused the injury
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I slipped on a wet floor in our own stockroom; nobody outside my company was involved.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: I just slipped on a wet floor in our stockroom — only my own company was involved.
scenarios.vetting_note: Outside-driver fact creates a separate negligence defendant; user has the fact but frames the problem as comp money.

scenarios.opening_query: I was injured on the job and my employer's insurance is handling it, but my hand may never work right again.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The press that crushed my hand had its safety guard fail; the manufacturer had recalled that guard model.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The machine's safety guard failed — turns out the manufacturer had recalled that guard.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether defective equipment made by an outside company caused the injury
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The press worked fine; my glove got caught because I was rushing at the end of a double shift.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The machine was fine — my glove got caught while I was rushing.
scenarios.vetting_note: Defective-product fact adds a products-liability defendant alongside comp; classic third-party pattern from the taxonomy description.

scenarios.opening_query: Hurt my back working and the comp payments are late every month. Do I need a lawyer?
scenarios.hidden_fact: I was rear-ended by a drunk driver while making deliveries in the company van.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: A drunk driver rear-ended me while I was out doing deliveries in the company van.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the on-duty injury was caused by an outside driver
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I strained it lifting pallets in our own warehouse; no one else was involved.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: I hurt it lifting pallets in the warehouse — nobody else involved.
scenarios.vetting_note: On-duty car crash is the textbook third-party case; opening reads as pure comp administration.

scenarios.opening_query: My workers' comp claim was accepted but I feel like I'm not getting everything I should for this injury.
scenarios.hidden_fact: An electrician from a different contractor dropped a conduit bundle on me at the shared job site.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: A guy from a different contractor dropped a bundle of conduit on me at the site.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether another company's employee caused the injury
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: My own foreman's rigging came loose; everyone involved works for my employer.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It was our own rigging that came loose — all my company's people.
scenarios.vetting_note: Multi-employer construction sites are where comp-only intuitions most often miss a third-party claim.

scenarios.opening_query: I've been off work since my accident and the insurance company keeps cutting my time-loss checks.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The accident happened when a customer's dog attacked me on a service call at their house.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: A customer's dog attacked me while I was on a service call at their home.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a person or animal owner outside the employer caused the injury
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I fell off our shop ladder; it was just worn out and my employer owns it.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: I fell off our own shop ladder — it was just worn out.
scenarios.vetting_note: Dog-owner liability is a third-party route users don't connect to their comp claim.

scenarios.opening_query: Work injury from last spring. Comp is paying medical but I lost my overtime and my knee is permanent.
scenarios.hidden_fact: A forklift operator from the staffing agency next door pinned my leg against the rack.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: A forklift driver from the staffing agency next door pinned my leg against the racking.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the injury was caused by someone not employed by the user's employer
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: One of my own crew clipped me with our forklift; we all work for the same company.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It was our own forklift and our own driver — same company as me.
scenarios.vetting_note: Coworker vs. outside-worker distinction is exactly the fact that flips exclusive-remedy analysis.

scenarios.opening_query: I'm a home health aide and I got seriously hurt on a shift. The comp process is overwhelming me.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The client's staircase railing was rotted and gave way, which is how I fell two floors.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The client's stair railing was rotted through and gave way under me.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a property owner's dangerous condition caused the work injury
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I hurt my shoulder lifting the client the way we always do; nothing was broken or unsafe in the home.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: I hurt my shoulder doing a normal lift — nothing was wrong with the house.
scenarios.vetting_note: Premises defect at a client's home adds a liability defendant while comp covers the employment side.

scenarios.opening_query: On workers' comp for a burn injury. My employer is being decent but I'm worried about the long term.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The burn came from a solvent drum that was mislabeled by the chemical supplier.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The drum was mislabeled by the chemical company that supplied it — that's why I got burned.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a supplier or manufacturer's error caused the injury
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I splashed the solvent on myself transferring it; the labeling and equipment were all correct.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: I just splashed it on myself while pouring — the labels were right.
scenarios.vetting_note: Supplier mislabeling is a concrete, checkable third-party fact rather than a role change.

scenarios.opening_query: Injured at work six weeks ago. HR filed the claim for me but nobody explains anything and I'm still in pain.
scenarios.hidden_fact: A subcontractor's crane load swung into the scaffold I was on.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: A subcontractor's crane load swung into my scaffold — that's what knocked me off.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether an outside contractor's operations caused the injury
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Our own scaffold plank snapped; my employer built and owns the scaffold.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Our own scaffold plank snapped — my company built it.
scenarios.vetting_note: Same accident, different responsible entity; only the disclosure separates the labels.

scenarios.opening_query: My husband is on comp after his accident at the plant and we think the settlement they're offering is too low.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The maintenance company that services the conveyor had skipped the inspection that would have caught the failure.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The outside maintenance company skipped the conveyor inspection — that's why it failed on him.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether an outside service company's negligence caused the accident
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The conveyor was maintained in-house by his own plant crew; no outside company touches it.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: His own plant crew maintains that conveyor — no outside company involved.
scenarios.vetting_note: Spouse framing plus low-settlement worry mirrors row 28's 'what are my options' posture.

scenarios.opening_query: I was hit by a car and badly hurt. The driver's insurance company keeps calling me about a settlement.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Personal Injury || Workers' Comp > State
scenarios.hidden_fact: I was on the clock delivering pizzas for my job when the car hit me.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: I was working — delivering pizzas on the clock when it happened.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the injured person was working at the time of the crash
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I was walking to the store on my day off when the car hit me.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: I was just walking to the store on my day off.
scenarios.vetting_note: On-the-clock status adds the comp claim; users describe the crash, not their duty status.

scenarios.opening_query: A dog attacked me last month and I needed surgery on my arm. I want to hold the owner responsible.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Personal Injury || Workers' Comp > State
scenarios.hidden_fact: It happened while I was reading meters on my route for the utility company.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: I was on my meter-reading route for work when the dog got me.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the attack occurred in the course of employment
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I was out on a personal evening walk in my neighborhood.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: I was just on my evening walk near home.
scenarios.vetting_note: Mirrors scenario 05 from the other side; employment context is the hidden fact.

scenarios.opening_query: I slipped on ice in a parking lot and shattered my wrist. The property company won't return my calls.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Premises Liability || Workers' Comp > State
scenarios.hidden_fact: It was my employer's lot and I was arriving for my scheduled shift.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It was the lot at my job — I was coming in for my shift.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whose property it was and whether the fall was connected to work
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It was the grocery store's lot; I was there shopping on a Saturday.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It was the grocery store's parking lot — I was just shopping.
scenarios.vetting_note: Paper failure mode injury_location: identical fall, different legal system depending on the employment nexus.

scenarios.opening_query: I fell off a ladder and broke my hip. Someone told me I could sue but I don't know who or how.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Personal Injury || Workers' Comp > State
scenarios.hidden_fact: The fall happened at the warehouse where I work, during my shift.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It happened at the warehouse where I work, right in the middle of my shift.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: where the fall happened and whether the person was working
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I was helping my neighbor clean his gutters as a favor when his ladder gave out.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: I was helping my neighbor with his gutters when his ladder gave out.
scenarios.vetting_note: Directly reproduces legacy_162 / the paper's injury_location case with a realistic disclosure.

scenarios.opening_query: My knee was wrecked in a car accident and the other driver was clearly at fault. I need representation.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Personal Injury || Workers' Comp > Third Party Litigation
scenarios.hidden_fact: I was driving between job sites in the company truck when they ran the light.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: I was in the company truck heading between job sites when they ran the light.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the crash happened while working
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I was driving my own car home from a family dinner.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: I was in my own car coming home from dinner with family.
scenarios.vetting_note: Work status converts a pure PI case into the comp-plus-third-party route — both parts change.

scenarios.opening_query: I got food poisoning so bad I was hospitalized for a week. Can I do anything about the restaurant?
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Personal Injury || Workers' Comp > State
scenarios.hidden_fact: It was the catered lunch my employer required us to attend for mandatory training.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It was the catered lunch at our mandatory work training — we had to be there.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the exposure happened at a required work event
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It was a dinner out with friends at a restaurant I picked.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It was just dinner out with friends.
scenarios.vetting_note: Mandatory-work-event nexus is a genuinely non-obvious comp fact; keeps a third-party caterer angle for realism.

scenarios.opening_query: A shelf display collapsed on me at a big-box store and I have a herniated disc now. What should I do?
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Premises Liability || Workers' Comp > State
scenarios.hidden_fact: I work at that store; it fell on me while I was restocking it on shift.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: I actually work there — it came down while I was restocking on my shift.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the injured person was a customer or on-duty employee when it fell
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I was a customer reaching for an item when the display came down.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: I was just shopping and reached for an item when it collapsed.
scenarios.vetting_note: The employment relationship is the user's own known fact, but the opening genuinely reads as a customer claim; disclosure is natural, not a role trick, because the store setting fits both.

scenarios.opening_query: I've had severe repetitive strain in both wrists for a year and my doctor says I need surgery I can't afford.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Personal Injury || Workers' Comp > State
scenarios.hidden_fact: My doctor traced it to my data-entry job; I type eight hours a day and my employer knows about it.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The doctor says it's from my data-entry job — I type all day and my employer knows.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the condition is work-caused
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It started after a bad bicycle crash on a poorly marked trail last summer.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It all started with a bike crash on a badly marked trail.
scenarios.vetting_note: Occupational-disease claims routinely arrive framed as medical/financial problems, not comp claims.

scenarios.opening_query: I have permanent hearing loss and ringing in my ears and I want to know if anyone is liable.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Personal Injury || Workers' Comp > State
scenarios.hidden_fact: Twenty years running grinders at the fabrication shop caused it, according to my audiologist.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: My audiologist says it's from twenty years on the grinders at the fab shop.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the hearing loss is occupational
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It happened at a concert venue where the pyrotechnics went off right next to my section.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Pyrotechnics went off right next to me at a concert.
scenarios.vetting_note: Occupational hearing loss is one of the most common late-filed comp claims; cause is the hidden fact.

scenarios.opening_query: My son was killed in an equipment accident and our family needs answers about what we can do.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Wrongful Death || Workers' Comp > State
scenarios.hidden_fact: He was operating his employer's excavator on a job when it rolled.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He was working — running his employer's excavator on a job site when it rolled.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the fatal accident happened in the course of employment
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: He was using a rented excavator on his own property on a weekend when it rolled.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: He'd rented the machine for his own land — it happened on his property.
scenarios.vetting_note: Fatal work accidents route survivors to comp death benefits first; heavy but realistic intake.
